author: @nidavis
text: I've long thought about making removable windmill-type vanes for the wheel of an e-bike and using the regenerative braking to charge the battery.

author: @nickhall8192
text: Hi Robert, not seen any of your videos for a while for some reason!? I had a thought about wind generators that you may be able to conceive an engineering solution. That is to change gear ratio as wind speed rises and drops. Is it possible?

author: @dannycallinan6745
text: Hey Robert I want to build a solar powered charging system that charges to supercapacitors then have then have the super caps charge a large battery like a 200a or something but I want it to start charging the battery when the supercaps are at peak volts and switch off when they drop to 10 volts then switch back on at peak volts and repeat the cycle can you or and one in the forums help me with the part im looking for?

author: @ConvairDart106
text: So what? You produced 14 volts. How many amps did you produce? That, is more important.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: watch the earlier videos in this series - and it is not more important it is as important

author: @largemarge1603
text:
I fabricated something similar in the early-1970s.
I could spin easy enough, but the instant I keyed the microphone on a CB radio, the drag slayed me.
That tiny four-Watt generation was beyond my ability to pedal.

author: @cosmiccrunch8591
text: Nice matrix bike! :D

author: @karlmyers6518
text:
Takes me back a long time too a concrete flywheel.  
Use the frame to Jack the rear end up and quick release flywheels with magnets. I wonder how much you could make like that.

author: @branni6538
text: Robert the hand cranks can be powered without the bike and still using leg power and peddles. A simple square wave double hand crank used with feet instead of hands and fixed at the other end opposite the generator would work. Just a double edition of the single hand crank drill.        Like this: ...../````\___/````

replies.author: @branni6538
replies.text: It would be a simple smithing job with a hammer and forge to shape a length of re-bar and connect to generator.

author: @DoubleBob
text:
How would you store energy? (That cement flywheel maybe?)
How does one hook up some phone charging bank to it?
I know it might be super trivial to some, but I don't know what I don't know and want to avoid any kind of gotchas, if I try to build something similar.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I'll do a video on it mate

author: @happydrones9187
text:
Dig the exercise.
Dynamo perked my interest..
Take an extra rim and tire, glue a car belt gear to it to get the gearing advantages, and set the generator on the ground off to the side..

author: @Machiuka
text: Nice tutorial mate. Maybe you make one about how to build a cheap 12V stationar solar battery (not Li-Ion or alike because those are not cheap). This will be also a solution for power outage and it is in your area of expertise where you are the best. Cheers.

author: @Stuartrusty
text:
As a seasoned engineer and cyclist of some 40 years and having done many endurance rides, I think that's about right. Over 7 hours I can average between 120-160 watts (I have a power meter crank arm on my bicycle to measure my wattage output). 
What (watt!) you have done there is easily attainable for most folk, even with the inefficiencies and losses of the drivetrain & generator, this setup will produce a more than useful power output to recharge large emergency storage LiFePo cells. A brilliant solution Mr. R.M.S.

author: @markhodgson2348
text: Energy in storms

author: @davidoneill2466
text: BRAVO BRAVO BRAVO……..

author: @iami9307
text: Also the two chains cannot coexist beside each other. That means the gear next to the one that has a generator connected to it is not usable they collide with each other

replies.author: @metralla
replies.text: It's hard to say but I think what would happen is that you will hear a weird noise and then remember you can't use the 1st gear

replies.author: @iami9307
replies.text: @metralla The generator chain would be on the first gear and the second gear is not usable. The gears are too close together and everything will lock up when they mesh and damage will occur.

replies.author: @iami9307
replies.text: @metralla there is an adjustment screw on the derailer that could be set to stop it from going beyond third gear and not going on second gear at all

replies.author: @iami9307
replies.text: @metralla I did that and decided to go to a water wheel instead. I used the 52 tooth sprocket off the front of the bike on the water wheel and five sets of sprockets removed from the back wheel which one is on the motor in case I want to add a second motor to drive off of it. The revolution is 60 to 1 and 120 on the second motor if used.

author: @iami9307
text: Excellent! Also a dynamo or anything on the tire will where the tire out rather quickly

author: @mikegb1969
text: solent green anybody bike trianer and a ebay motor

author: @huckmiddeke665
text:
Excellent approach and if we consider the UK overshoot day (19.5.) a national emergency, as we actually need to upgrade our footprint to ~⅓ of what it is now, then this should be taken into use in every household.
I'm muscle powering my own electricity and it's really not that hard. It's also easy to store energy, available on demand and based on recycled materials and easy low-tech, which is the whole opposite of building big scale new photovoltaic or windpower, which is still based on fossil energy and resources for manufacturing and maintenance. Thank you once again. Could you show a "how to convert any ebike to be used as a generator"?

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Also if you add in the dynamo principal then you add in a lot of friction which reduces the output substantially. Love the frame setup and hooking up directly to the chain with the auxiliary gearbox. Excellent video Rob

author: @Tom_From_Oz
text: With a storm brewing would one of your propeller replacements hooked up to the generator do it a little easier and then switch it back to the tricycle when the wind has died down?

replies.author: @justinweatherford8129
replies.text: One would have to match the turbine type with the wind speed. If it were not sturdy enough then it might break. If it were made too sturdy then it might not turn at all.

replies.author: @nidavis
replies.text: ​ @Justin Weatherford  or just use the existing wheels and make vanes that clip onto the spokes to turn the rear wheels from the wind?

replies.author: @justinweatherford8129
replies.text: @nidavis what I was saying was built to remain intact during a class 5 tornado wouldn't generate any electricity when the wind speeds are only 5mph, and a turbine built to generate electricity when wind speeds are 5 mph would be very likely to be torn apart from any tornado force winds. One has to match a turbine for the expected wind speeds.

author: @AlanAlanAlanDave
text: As far as creating emergency power, will this work: Connect a single phase, 220v, 50Hz, 1CV, 2,800rpm electric motor via matching pulleys & belt to a 24v, 300amp lorry alternator (modified by cutting off the triangle parts of the rotor and adding say, 80x20x10mm neodymium magnets in alternate north south configuration creating a brushless motor) then, wire the alternator output to a multi-plug extension lead and plug the single phase motor in to one of its sockets. Wind a cord around the shaft of the alternator (opposite end to the pulley) to act as a pull cord and, pull to spin the alternator. Thus generating enough electricity to power the electric motor which in turn, spins the shaft of the alternator via the belt to generate power for not only the motor but, also other electrical items plugged into the remaining socket outlets.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: It all comes down to the torque needed to turn the motor and how fast you're able to turn it. Then how long you're able to keep up the speed. These factors determine the power you're able to get out of the motor. Also if it's induction, does it need a charge on the secondary winding.

author: @TabooRevolution13
text: Yo Robert Murray Smith...  let's be realistic about this. The bike is good and I've used the bike in a jam but what we want for emergency power is steam power with a Tesla turbine! Nobody is really making any decent ones yet but there are a few for sale around 120 bucks...  they put out 9 volts or so. If you put two together you'd have a working voltage at 18!

author: @twestgard2
text: Is this intended to repeat the scene in Soylent Green where Charlton Heston powers the TV with a bicycle? Because this looks almost exactly like the scene in Soylent Green where Charlton Heston powers the TV with a bicycle.

replies.author: @wongowonga
replies.text: That's where we are headed at this rate.

author: @TrollingAround
text: Sugar in your tea converted into electricity to boil the kettle to make more tea?

author: @TabooRevolution13
text: So if you had two wheels spinning you'd have twice the momentum if you turned them into flywheels with concrete!  Imagine if you had 10 concrete wheels on each side! It might be hard to get it going but leg power has a lot of potential if you use it right. I think it's a matter of amplifying that leg power then with capacitors and bigger sprockets and flywheels...   A road bike with a big sprocket with a concrete flywheel like the ones you make is what we want I think. Some of those Maxwell size ultra-capacitors they use to start Semi Trucks to charge a battery bank through a big blocking diode would be good!
